page: Amazon Web Services c8g.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the custom AWS Neoverse-V2 processor built on the arm64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz, with no hyperthreading (1.0 thread per core). The CPU cache configuration includes 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the c8g.8xlarge demonstrates strong memory performance and multi-threaded efficiency. Its memory subsystem achieves top-tier results, with uncached read-only large block bandwidth reaching 391.61 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 171.76 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is highlighted by a Geekbench multi-core score of 21709.0 and a Passmark database operations score of 46834.4, both placing in the top tier. Decompression tasks are highly efficient, with single-threaded bzip3 decompression reaching 14.43 GB/sec. However, large-scale LLM inference presents a performance tradeoff, as prompt processing for a 70B parameter model drops to a weak 5.89 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the arm64 architecture and dedicated core allocation provide high computational density and predictable performance without the overhead of hyperthreading. This makes the c8g.8xlarge suitable for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, database operations, high-throughput static web serving, and multi-threaded data decompression. It is also applicable for medium-scale machine learning inference and general-purpose compute tasks that require high memory bandwidth but do not require local storage or GPU acceleration.
description: The Amazon Web Services c8g.8xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized server powered by a 32-core AWS Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs with a 1:1 physical-to-virtual core ratio, 64.0 GB of RAM, and a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance delivers top-tier memory bandwidth, reaching 391.61 GB/sec for uncached reads, and strong multi-core performance, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 21709.0 and high database operation throughput. While it exhibits lower performance on large 70B LLM inference tasks, its high memory bandwidth and dedicated compute resources make it efficient for software compilation, database workloads, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.8xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized server featuring 32 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 64 GB of RAM, and 15 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.8xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized server with 32 dedicated cores, 64 GB RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth.
tagline: An arm64 compute-optimized platform delivering dedicated physical cores, top-tier memory bandwidth, and high-throughput multi-threaded performance for demanding workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated AWS Neoverse-V2 arm64 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core
Top-tier memory bandwidth with 391.61 GB/sec uncached read speeds
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for software compilation, database operations, and web serving
Lacks local storage and GPU acceleration, limiting large-scale AI training
